title: juglans microcarpa subsp. major
intro: Juglans microcarpa subsp. major, also known as Mexican walnut or Mexican evergreen walnut, is a subspecies of evergreen tree that belongs to the walnut family (Juglandaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, due to the presence of tannins, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Juglans microcarpa subsp. major include its potential to act as an antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent, which may help in reducing the risk of certain diseases and improving overall well-being. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its leaves in hot water, and it can also be consumed as a tea, although its use should be limited to traditional and well-researched contexts.
